description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array, consisting of a series of dark photovoltaic panels arranged in a single, linear row. The panels are supported by a robust metal framework, which is anchored to the ground with concrete bases at regular intervals. The surface of the panels is smooth and glossy, reflecting light, and the structure is elevated above the ground to allow for airflow and maintenance access. The mounting system appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing both strength and corrosion resistance. The array is designed for outdoor installation, typical for solar farms or urban renewable energy installations. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images, and the array is not intended for direct human interaction beyond maintenance.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a linear row of glossy, dark photovoltaic panels supported by a metal frame and concrete bases. The structure is elevated and designed for outdoor solar energy generation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the solar panel array, with all panels and supporting posts visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the panels and the depth of the array, with two supporting posts and concrete bases visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panels, the supporting frame, and the mounting posts, with no visible cabling or inverters.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the tilt and structure from the other end.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metal posts and concrete bases
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 12 panels
